business.name: Northline Heating & Cooling
business.tagline: Comfort that lasts. Service that shows up.
business.oneLineDescription: Family-owned HVAC repair, installation, and maintenance for homes across the west Denver metro — same-week service, technicians who explain the work, and honest upfront pricing.

contact.serviceArea: Wheat Ridge, Denver, Arvada, Golden, Lakewood & the west Denver metro

audience.customerDescription: Homeowners in the west Denver metro who want a comfortable house without the runaround — clear pricing, technicians who explain what they're doing, and systems that keep working long after the truck leaves.

topQuestions.question: How much does a new furnace or AC installation cost?
topQuestions.answer: Most full system replacements in our area run between $6,500 and $14,000 installed, depending on the size of your home, the efficiency rating you choose, and whether ductwork needs modifying. We give you a fixed, itemized quote before any work starts — no surprises, and no pressure to buy more system than your home needs.

topQuestions.question: How fast can you come out for a repair?
topQuestions.answer: We reserve same-week appointments for repairs and run a 24/7 emergency line for no-heat and no-cool situations. Most non-emergency service calls are scheduled within 1–2 business days, and we give you a two-hour arrival window so you're not waiting around all day.

topQuestions.question: Do you service my area?
topQuestions.answer: We cover Wheat Ridge, Denver, Arvada, Golden, Lakewood, and the surrounding west Denver metro. If you're just outside that range, call us — we'll tell you honestly whether we can help or point you to someone who can.

topQuestions.question: Are your technicians licensed, insured, and background-checked?
topQuestions.answer: Yes. Northline is fully licensed (CO HVAC #4471) and insured, every technician is NATE-certified and background-checked, and we're a factory-authorized Carrier dealer. The person in your home is a trained employee — never a day-labor sub.

topQuestions.question: Do you offer financing and maintenance plans?
topQuestions.answer: We offer approved financing on new system installs with monthly payments, and our Comfort Club maintenance plan covers two tune-ups a year, priority scheduling, and a discount on repairs. Both are optional — we'll walk you through the numbers so you can decide.

services.name: Heating & furnace repair
services.description: Fast, accurate diagnosis and repair for furnaces, heat pumps, and boilers — most fixes done in a single visit, with the failed part shown to you and the cost agreed before we start.

services.name: Air conditioning service & install
services.description: AC tune-ups, repairs, and high-efficiency system replacements sized correctly for your home so you get even cooling without sky-high summer bills.

services.name: Preventative maintenance
services.description: Seasonal tune-ups that catch small problems before they become no-heat emergencies and keep your manufacturer warranty valid.

services.name: Indoor air quality
services.description: Whole-home humidifiers, filtration, and air purification for households dealing with dry winters, allergies, and Front Range dust.

services.name: Emergency service
services.description: 24/7 response for no-heat and no-cool situations, because a broken furnace at 11pm in January can't wait for business hours.

testimonials.quote: Our furnace died during the first cold snap and Northline had a tech at the house before noon the next day. He showed me the cracked part, quoted the fix on the spot, and had heat back on within the hour.
testimonials.author: Danielle R.

testimonials.quote: We got three quotes for a new AC and Northline was the only one who actually measured the house instead of upselling us. Two summers in and the upstairs is finally cool.
testimonials.author: Marcus T.

testimonials.quote: The Comfort Club plan is worth it just for the peace of mind. They catch things early and we've never had a surprise breakdown since we signed up.
testimonials.author: Priya & Sam K.
